config.rationale: Next highest-value point is an SGLang A/B against the successful baseline, changing only MTP/NEXTN so the throughput delta is attributable. I kept tp=1, mem_fraction_static=0.8, fcfs, prefix caching, and max_model_len=65536 identical to experiment 44b82ae8a640; that gives the cleanest measurement for optimize_throughput and avoids the confound from jumping straight to 262144 context allocation. On a single 80GB H100 with a 9B Qwen-family model, NEXTN with 3 speculative steps and 4 draft tokens is a reasonable first MTP point: expected gain is roughly +15% to +30% throughput on decode-heavy traffic, with little TTFT improvement and possible slight regression on short outputs or prefill-heavy mixes. For the user’s exact 262144-context command, my estimate is lower, about +8% to +20% net throughput versus non-speculative SGLang at the same 262144 context, because the much larger KV reservation reduces batching headroom and can eat part of the speculative win; that is why I would benchmark NEXTN at 65536 first, then move to 131072, and only then 262144 if headroom remains. For vLLM, --language-model-only on this VLM checkpoint is likely a smaller lever than MTP: expected throughput gain is usually ~0% to +8% at fixed context/concurrency, mainly from freeing vision-component memory and startup overhead rather than changing decode math; the upside can reach ~10% to +15% only if the saved VRAM lets vLLM admit a meaningfully larger KV cache or higher batch/concurrency. This config is the best next experiment because it directly tests the most likely throughput win before spending runs on cross-engine tuning.

llm_commentary: The latest run failed outright, so it delivered no usable throughput or latency and is worse than the baseline on all three goals. Against throughput, it does not challenge 44b82ae8a640's 4395.5 tok/s; against latency, it provides no valid TTFT and remains behind the 142.4 ms leader; for the balanced goal, it is non-competitive because it produced neither throughput nor a sub-500 ms TTFT result. The result is still informative because it suggests this SGLang NEXTN/MTP configuration is currently unstable or misconfigured, so the next step should focus on recovering a valid speculative decoding measurement before moving to larger context lengths.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-sglang-0dde5caf3978 --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d -e SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2=1 lmsysorg/sglang:latest python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tp-size 1 --dtype auto --enable-metrics --mem-fraction-static 0.9 --context-length 65536 --mamba-scheduler-strategy extra_buffer --speculative-eagle-topk 1 --speculative-algorithm NEXTN --speculative-num-steps 3 --reasoning-parser qwen3 --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder
